other_observers: I observed it with Larry Semo, Steve Mlodinow, Joel & Marcel Such, Jim & Karen Schmoker, et al.; found by Larry Semo & Steve Mlodinow
specific_location: Golden Ponds (one pond west of main pond in front of parking lot), Longmont
description: A predominantly blackish and brownish Aythya duck with a prominent tuft. Bill mostly bluish-gray with a large black tip. Head brownish black with yellow eye. Tuft much more prominent than LESC, swept back to overhang the nape and variable in prominence depending on wind direction and state of relaxation. Flanks brownish gray, with lighter tips on flank feathers. Tail dark. Wings with very wide white stripe visible on upper surface, mostly whitish underneath with dark leading edge. Belly and lower breast whitish. Legs seen to be unbanded with halluxes intact.Behaviors: Alternated between active feeding, sleeping, and resting on the ice with a little bit of preening now and then. Made a few short flights from one end of the open water (rest of pond frozen) to the other.Call: not heardPlumage: adult female or immature male
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
